query: What is the capital of France?

France shares land borders with Germany, Spain, and Italy.

Mount Everest is the tallest mountain above sea level.

query: How do plants convert sunlight into energy?

candidates.text: Photosynthesis occurs mainly in the chloroplasts of plant cells.
Solar panels convert sunlight into electricity using photovoltaic cells.
Deserts receive very little annual rainfall on average.
Chlorophyll absorbs light most strongly in the blue and red wavelengths.
Cellular respiration releases energy stored in glucose molecules.
Plants use chlorophyll to capture sunlight and convert it into chemical energy via photosynthesis.
The moon has no atmosphere to scatter sunlight.
Coral reefs rely on symbiotic algae for a portion of their energy.

query: What HTTP status code means the resource was not found?

candidates.text: HTTP 200 OK indicates the request succeeded.
HTTP 404 Not Found means the server could not locate the requested resource.
HTTP 500 Internal Server Error indicates a generic server-side failure.
TCP is a connection-oriented transport protocol.
REST APIs typically use JSON as the payload format.
HTTP 301 Moved Permanently redirects clients to a new URL.
DNS resolves domain names into IP addresses.
WebSockets provide full-duplex communication over a single TCP connection.

query: How is Mean Reciprocal Rank calculated in information retrieval?

candidates.text: Precision measures the fraction of retrieved documents that are relevant.
Recall measures the fraction of relevant documents that were retrieved.
NDCG discounts relevance scores logarithmically by rank position.
Mean Reciprocal Rank averages the reciprocal of the rank of the first relevant result across queries.
A confusion matrix summarizes classifier predictions versus ground truth.
F1 score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall.
Cross-validation splits data into folds to estimate generalization error.
Gradient descent iteratively updates parameters to minimize a loss function.

query: At what temperature does water boil at sea level?

candidates.text: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
Ice is less dense than liquid water, so it floats.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 Fahrenheit) at sea-level atmospheric pressure.
Boiling point decreases with altitude due to lower atmospheric pressure.
Steam carries more thermal energy than boiling water at the same temperature.
Salt dissolved in water raises its boiling point slightly.
The human body is roughly 60 percent water by weight.
Copper is an excellent conductor of both heat and electricity.

query: What does git rebase do differently from git merge?

candidates.text: git commit records a snapshot of staged changes to the repository.
git clone copies a remote repository to a local directory.
git status shows the state of the working directory and staging area.
git branch lists, creates, or deletes branches.
git stash temporarily shelves uncommitted changes.
git merge creates a new commit that joins two branch histories together.
git rebase replays commits from one branch onto another, producing a linear history instead of a merge commit.
git tag marks a specific commit, often for releases.

query: What is a vector embedding used for in semantic search?

candidates.text: A hash table maps keys to values via a hash function.
B-trees are commonly used for database indexing.
SQL joins combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column.
Regular expressions match patterns in text using a formal syntax.
A bloom filter is a space-efficient probabilistic membership test.
Tokenization splits text into words or subword units before model input.
Caching stores frequently accessed data to reduce latency.
A vector embedding maps text to a dense numeric vector so that semantically similar text ends up close together, enabling semantic search via nearest-neighbor lookup.

query: Why is the mitochondrion called the powerhouse of the cell?

candidates.text: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP through oxidative phosphorylation, earning the nickname powerhouse of the cell.
The nucleus houses the cell's genetic material in most eukaryotes.
Ribosomes translate messenger RNA into proteins.
The Golgi apparatus modifies and packages proteins for secretion.
Lysosomes contain enzymes that break down cellular waste.
The cell membrane regulates what enters and exits the cell.
Vacuoles store water, nutrients, and waste products in plant cells.
Endoplasmic reticulum synthesizes lipids and processes proteins.

query: What does inflation mean in economics?

candidates.text: GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced in a country.
Unemployment rate is the share of the labor force without a job but seeking one.
A central bank sets monetary policy, including interest rates.
A trade deficit occurs when imports exceed exports.
Inflation is the rate at which the general price level of goods and services rises over time, eroding purchasing power.
The stock market aggregates buyers and sellers of company shares.
A recession is commonly defined as two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth.
Fiscal policy refers to government spending and taxation decisions.

query: What is a JWT used for in web authentication?

candidates.text: OAuth 2.0 is an authorization framework for delegated access.
Cookies are small pieces of data stored by the browser on behalf of a website.
CORS controls which origins are allowed to make cross-origin requests.
A JWT (JSON Web Token) is a compact, signed token that encodes claims, commonly used to authenticate and authorize API requests without server-side session state.
TLS encrypts data in transit between a client and a server.
bcrypt is a password-hashing function designed to be slow to resist brute force.
CSRF tokens protect against cross-site request forgery attacks.
Rate limiting throttles the number of requests a client can make in a window.
